title: Zphyr Training Dataset v1
description: Schema for the Qwen fine-tuning training dataset. Distinct from Evals/datasets/schema.json (eval harness schema). Validates files in Evals/training/raw/ and Evals/training/splits/.

id.description: Globally unique stable identifier. Format: zphyr-{category}-{subcategory_short}-{NNN}. Never reuse or recycle IDs.

raw_asr_text.description: Exact Whisper output, unmodified. Should generally lack sentence-final punctuation (Whisper does not produce it). Non-null examples with terminal punctuation in raw will trigger a validator warning.

final_expected_text.description: Gold output: the correctly formatted text the model must produce. For null edits, must be byte-identical to raw_asr_text. Must not contain words absent from raw except structural tokens (punctuation, list markers, newlines).

category.description: Top-level category. See Evals/annotation/category_taxonomy.md for full descriptions and subcategory constraints.

subcategory.description: Subcategory. Must be valid for the given category. Validator checks category/subcategory combinations.

language.description: Primary language of the utterance (ISO 639-1). Use 'mixed' when the utterance contains significant content in two or more languages (code-switching, multilingual category).

rewrite_allowed_level.description: Maximum transformation the model is permitted to apply. Set this BEFORE writing final_expected_text. no_edit: raw == expected exactly. punctuation_only: adds sentence-final punct, capitalizes sentence start and clear proper nouns, executes spoken punct commands. light_polish: also allows filler word removal, word repetition collapse (when unambiguously artifactual), and list structure detection from explicit signals.

is_null_edit.description: true iff final_expected_text is byte-identical to raw_asr_text. Validator enforces this. Target: 12-15% of training set, 20% of seed set.

difficulty.description: Annotation complexity: easy (obvious, single operation), medium (requires one judgment call), hard (requires multiple judgment calls or has high risk of wrong decision).

severity_if_wrong.description: Impact of a model error on this example. critical: technical corruption or translation that would create a bug or misrepresentation. high: significant meaning change. medium: noticeable quality issue. low: minor cosmetic issue. See guidelines.md §7 for defaults by category.

source_type.description: How the example was created. hand_written: authored from scratch. semi_synthetic: clean target text corrupted to produce raw (e.g. punctuation stripped, fillers injected), then human-reviewed. synthetic: fully generated (prompt-generated candidate, human-reviewed). eval_derived: adapted from an existing Evals/datasets/ eval case (MUST go to test split only, not train).

protected_terms.description: Exact string spans that must survive verbatim in final_expected_text, checked case-sensitively. Required for all technical examples. Add any token where a character-level change creates an error: package names, env vars, URLs, paths, semver, proper-case brands. Validator checks each term appears in both raw_asr_text and final_expected_text.

no_translation.description: When true, no word or phrase may be translated between languages. Mandatory true for category=multilingual. Default assumption is true for all rows unless the example is specifically testing a future translation feature.

has_asr_artifact.description: true if the raw_asr_text contains at least one ASR-introduced artifact (filler, repetition, split word, wrong number, hallucination).

asr_artifact_types.description: Types of ASR artifacts present. filler_word: um/uh/euh/hm. repetition: ASR artifact word repeat. split_word: Whisper split a compound incorrectly. wrong_number: Whisper transcribed wrong number. hallucination: Whisper added words not spoken.

constraint_ids.description: Hard constraint IDs this example tests. CONSTRAINT-01 through CONSTRAINT-10. See guidelines.md §8. Recommended for high-severity examples. Used by build_hard_constraints.py to generate constraint-specific test subsets.

notes.description: Concise annotation rationale. Explain what is being tested, why the expected output is correct, and what a model might do wrong. Max 3 sentences.

annotation_confidence.description: Annotator certainty about final_expected_text. low: must be resolved (rewrite or reject) before inclusion in any split. medium: flag for second review. high: ready for approval.

acceptable_variants.description: Alternative gold outputs that are also correct, for eval tolerance. Each variant must be genuinely correct (not just plausible). Max 3. If more than 3 outputs are acceptable, the example is too ambiguous and should be simplified. Validator warns if a variant equals raw_asr_text (invalid for non-null examples) or equals final_expected_text (redundant).

review_status.description: Lifecycle status. Only 'approved' rows are eligible for splits. 'rejected' rows are moved to annotation/rejected/. 'needs_review' rows have annotation_confidence=low and must be resolved.

allOf.description: Multilingual examples must not allow translation
null_edits examples must use no_edit rewrite level

x-note: This schema applies to Evals/training/ files only. The eval harness schema (Evals/datasets/schema.json) is a different, separate schema for a different purpose. Do not mix them.
